name: Wes
manager_notes: Wes got promoted to manage the team he was on three months ago. Something's been off since — a couple of his former peers have gone quiet with him, and there was visible tension in last week's planning. He hasn't said anything's wrong. I don't want to frame this as him doing badly; the peer-to-manager jump is just hard. I want to open a no-pressure space to hear how the transition is actually landing and where the friction is.
answers: It's been a weird few months, not going to pretend otherwise.
A couple of people I used to grab lunch with are a bit distant now.
Planning last week got tense and I wasn't sure how to land it.
I don't want to come down heavy on people who were my mates.
I think they're still figuring out how to take direction from me.
Part of me misses just being one of the team.
I could use a steer on where to draw the line.
Mostly I want to get the trust back.
A bit of structure on how to lead the people I used to sit with would help.
